text.title: surveys Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the surveys methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Survey config MUST declare at least one standard instrument (NPS / CSAT / SUS / SEQ) OR a custom instrument with documented validity references.

rationale.source: SUS — Brooke 1996; CSAT — Bradburn et al.

rule.rationale: Standard instruments allow comparison across releases and against industry benchmarks.

rule.statement: Survey items MUST be single-attribute (no 'rate the price and quality of...'). Validator MUST flag obvious double-barrel patterns.

rationale.source: Dillman — Internet, Phone, Mail, and Mixed-Mode Surveys

rule.rationale: Double-barreled items conflate two attributes; respondents cannot give a coherent answer.

rule.statement: Survey items MUST be neutral. Loaded words ('amazing', 'awful', 'shouldn't we') MUST be replaced with neutral phrasing.
rule.rationale: Leading wording inflates positive or negative responses, invalidating the data.

rule.statement: Likert / ordinal scales MUST label every point (1=strongly disagree … 5=strongly agree). Unlabeled midpoints are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unlabeled points make respondents impose own meaning, increasing noise.

rule.statement: Config MUST declare an expected response-rate target (≥10% for email cold-list, ≥40% for in-app prompt, ≥70% for intercept). Final analysis MUST report actual response rate.
rule.rationale: Low response rates create non-response bias; surfacing the rate keeps analysis honest.

rule.statement: Analysis plan (cross-tabs, weighting, significance test) MUST be declared BEFORE fielding. Post-hoc analysis additions MUST be flagged as exploratory.
rule.rationale: Pre-declared plan prevents p-hacking and selective reporting.
